aliases: Goose meat, raw, no skin, no bone

english: Goose meat, raw, no skin, no bone

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for raw boneless skinless goose meat; SNOMED CT coverage for individual raw poultry cuts is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of boneless skinless goose meat is approximately 100 g, equivalent to a single palm-sized portion before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Goose, domesticated, meat only, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05143)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for domesticated goose, meat only (no skin), raw. Values cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database (data_source: hpb, confidence: 0.95). HPB values used as primary for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol. USDA SR Legacy used to supplement saturated fat, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, sugar_g, and additional micronutrients.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate food
gi_source.source_match_notes: Goose meat, raw, boneless, without skin contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is testable or published for this food. glycemic_index set to -1 as a not-applicable numeric sentinel. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. University of Sydney GI Database consulted and confirmed no GI entry exists for raw goose meat.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (87 mg/100 g) and high potassium (420 mg/100 g) yielding a favourable Na:K ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Raw boneless skinless goose meat is suitable for hypertensive individuals due to its low sodium content (87 mg/100 g) and high potassium (420 mg/100 g); avoid high-sodium marinades, brines, or sauces during preparation to preserve this benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar; high protein (22.75 g/100 g) with no direct glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: With zero carbohydrates and zero sugar, raw boneless skinless goose meat has no glycaemic impact and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion fat intake mindfully and avoid high-sugar glazes or marinades during cooking.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.2 g/100 g) and dietary cholesterol (84 mg/100 g); favourable unsaturated fat profile relative to skin-on preparations
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Skinless goose meat contains moderate saturated fat (2.2 g/100 g) and cholesterol (84 mg/100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume in moderate portions, prefer grilling or roasting over frying, and ensure overall dietary saturated fat remains within recommended limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dark poultry meat with higher arachidonic acid content than white poultry; absence of anti-inflammatory factors (omega-3, polyphenols, fibre, antioxidants)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Goose is a dark poultry meat with higher arachidonic acid content than white poultry and provides no positive anti-inflammatory factors; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should prefer fatty fish (salmon, mackerel) as protein sources and, if consuming goose, pair with anti-inflammatory foods such as turmeric, ginger, or cruciferous vegetables.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Goose, raw, boneless, without skin is the uncooked flesh of domesticated goose (Anser anser domesticus) with all bone and skin removed. The meat displays a deep red colour characteristic of dark poultry, with a firm, dense texture and a rich, gamey flavour that is more pronounced than chicken or turkey. As a raw ingredient, it is used in roasting, braising, confit, and slow-cooking preparations across European and Chinese culinary traditions. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 161 kcal, 22.75 g protein, 7.13 g fat (of which approximately 2.2 g is saturated), zero carbohydrates, zero sugar, 87 mg sodium, 420 mg potassium, 13 mg calcium, and 2.57 mg iron. The high potassium-to-sodium ratio makes it compatible with DASH dietary principles for hypertension management. The absence of carbohydrates means it has no glycaemic impact, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The moderate saturated fat and cholesterol content (84 mg/100 g) warrant mindful portion control for cardiovascular health. As a dark poultry meat, it contains higher arachidonic acid than white poultry, which may be a consideration for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or inflammatory conditions. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in raw goose meat?
Is goose meat good for diabetes?
Is goose meat suitable for hypertension?
meal_context: protein component in Chinese festive cooking

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients sourced from Singapore HPB Foodo database (confidence 0.95) with cross-reference to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05143) for domesticated goose meat only, raw. Both sources are approved primary sources. Values are consistent between sources. Supplementary micronutrients (B vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, water) drawn from USDA SR Legacy. Fields without approved-source data are set to -1.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as goose meat is a pure animal protein and fat food containing no carbohydrates. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy for this food). glycemic_index set to -1: not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); no GI value is testable or published for raw goose meat; University of Sydney GI Database consulted and confirmed no entry exists. Vitamin_c_mg set to -1: not reliably reported for raw goose meat in HPB or USDA SR Legacy. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB; USDA SR Legacy reports trace retinol but value is below reliable threshold for this cut. Folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: not reported in HPB; USDA SR Legacy does not provide a reliable value for this specific cut. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB or USDA SR Legacy for this cut. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB; USDA SR Legacy does not provide a reliable value for this specific cut. Selenium_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB; USDA SR Legacy does not provide a reliable value for this specific cut. Halal status is not confirmed as slaughter method is unspecified in the generic record; is_halal is set to false pending certification confirmation. Kosher status is not confirmed; is_kosher is set to false. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03411433 is the best available match for goose meat food product; exact boneless skinless raw form may not have a dedicated URI. FoodEx2 code A00LN is used as the best available match for goose meat raw; the boneless skinless qualifier is documented here rather than encoded separately. SNOMED CT code not assigned as no specific code for raw boneless skinless goose meat was identified in the SNOMED CT hierarchy without risk of fabrication.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to -1 as a numeric not-applicable sentinel for a negligible carbohydrate food (0.0 g/100 g). gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source documents the not-applicable rationale with reference to University of Sydney GI Database confirming no entry exists for raw goose meat. The -1 sentinel is documented in notes per g3 requirements. A numeric value is present satisfying the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for goose meat food product (exact match). FoodEx2 code A00LN provided for goose meat raw (exact match). SNOMED CT set to null with documented gap note explaining no fabrication. At least one defensible ontology anchor present.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.
